{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Chigorin Defence| |rd| |bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | |nd| | | | | |= | | | |pd| | | | |= | | |pl|pl| | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl| | |pl|pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= |moves=1.d4 d5 2.c4 Nc6 |eco=[[Chess/ECOD|D07]] }} =Chigorin Defence= ===2...Nc6=== With 2...Nc6 Black does not support the central d5 pawn but develops a piece for an uneven game. Theory in this defence has been extended in recent years in part due to the attempt to create playable lines in the [[Chess/Black Knights' Tango|Black Knights' Tango]] which can transpose into a Chigorin Defence. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}.
